title: Support null ports in non-ANSI module declaration
body:
Using the head version of Verilator, the following module fails:
```
module t5(a,);
input a;
endmodule
```
This yields:
```
$ ~/git/verilator/bin/verilator --lint-only t5.v 
%Error: t5.v:1:13: syntax error, unexpected ')', expecting '['
    1 | module t5(a,);
      |             ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```
While not particularly pretty, this is allowed as seen both [here](https://www.accellera.org/images/eda/sv-bc/10162.html) as well as here #321 (which implies this is expected to work)

This is yet another fight against 3rd party IP (this time from a giant CPU vendor codebase).
